name: Alcazaba de Reina y Ciudad romana Regina Turdulorum — circular por la Vía Verde Mina Jayona
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Fuente Del Arco are situated in the foothills of the Sierra Morena mountains, offering a diverse landscape for cyclists. The region is characterized by varied terrain, including agricultural tracks, local roads, and dedicated vías pecuarias, providing a mix of surfaces and scenery. Cyclists can explore areas within the Guadiana and Guadalquivir river basins, alongside the mountainous backdrop of the Sierra del Viento. This environment features numerous springs and natural formations, making it a compelling destination for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Fuente Del Arco The most popular touring cycling route is Alcazaba de Reina y Ciudad romana Regina Turdulorum — circular por la Vía Verde Mina Jayona, a 25.3 miles (40.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 50 minutes to complete. This route combines historical sites with a section of the Vía Verde Mina Jayona. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Hermitage of the Virgin of Ara – Alcazaba de Reina loop from Guadalcanal, a difficult 46.3 miles (74.6 km) path. This route connects significant historical landmarks, offering extensive views across the landscape. Local touring cyclists also love the Llerena – Plaza de España loop from Casas de Reina, a 22.9 miles (36.9 km) trail leading through the rural surroundings of Llerena, often completed in about 3 hours 11 minutes. Touring cycling around Fuente Del Arco is defined by mountainous foothills, river basin areas, and a network of agricultural tracks and local roads. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging routes. The routes in Fuente Del Arco are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 63 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Fuente Del Arco's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Fuente Del Arco?
A: There are 8 touring cycling routes around Fuente Del Arco, offering a range of experiences from moderate to difficult. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Fuente Del Arco?
A: The terrain around Fuente Del Arco is quite diverse, situated in the foothills of the Sierra Morena mountains. You'll encounter a mix of agricultural tracks, local roads, senderos (trails), and vías pecuarias (livestock trails). Routes often traverse areas within the Guadiana and Guadalquivir river basins, providing varied scenery and surfaces.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling options in Fuente Del Arco?
A: Yes, the region offers routes suitable for families. The presence of a 20 km Vía Verde (Greenway) extending to Azuaga provides dedicated, traffic-free paths with gentle gradients, ideal for relaxed touring cycling. A good example is the section of the Vía Verde included in the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021939627/alcazaba-de-reina-y-ciudad-romana-regina-turdulorum-circular-por-la-via-verde-mina-jayona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcazaba de Reina y Ciudad romana Regina Turdulorum — circular por la Vía Verde Mina Jayona&lt;/a&gt; route, which is rated as moderate.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I visit while cycling in Fuente Del Arco?
A: You can combine your cycling with visits to several historical and natural landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5326622" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgin of Ara&lt;/a&gt;, known as the 'Sistine Chapel of Extremadura,' is a significant cultural stop. You can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5423343" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman city of Regina Turdulorum&lt;/a&gt; and enjoy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5332635" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;views of Castillo de Reina&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021939627/alcazaba-de-reina-y-ciudad-romana-regina-turdulorum-circular-por-la-via-verde-mina-jayona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcazaba de Reina y Ciudad romana Regina Turdulorum — circular por la Vía Verde Mina Jayona&lt;/a&gt; route specifically combines historical sites with cycling.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Fuente Del Arco?
A: The best seasons for touring cycling in Fuente Del Arco are autumn, winter, and spring. The climate during these months allows for comfortable exploration. It is advisable to avoid the summer months due to high temperatures.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021939627/alcazaba-de-reina-y-ciudad-romana-regina-turdulorum-circular-por-la-via-verde-mina-jayona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcazaba de Reina y Ciudad romana Regina Turdulorum — circular por la Vía Verde Mina Jayona&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular route. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14621306" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llerena – Plaza de España loop from Casas de Reina&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through rural surroundings.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the cycling trails in Fuente Del Arco?
A: While the region offers many natural trails, specific regulations for dogs on cycling trails can vary. It's always recommended to check local signage and rules for each specific trail or natural area, such as the Mina La Jayona Natural Monument, to ensure dog-friendly access.

Q: Are there any unique natural attractions to see while cycling?
A: Absolutely. A standout natural attraction is the Mina La Jayona Natural Monument, an old iron mine that has been reclaimed by nature. It features an impressive central void, a unique microclimate, and filtering light, creating a magical atmosphere. While touring by bike, this offers a fascinating stop to explore. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021939627/alcazaba-de-reina-y-ciudad-romana-regina-turdulorum-circular-por-la-via-verde-mina-jayona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcazaba de Reina y Ciudad romana Regina Turdulorum — circular por la Vía Verde Mina Jayona&lt;/a&gt; route incorporates a section of the Vía Verde near this area.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of touring cycling routes in Fuente Del Arco?
A: The routes in Fuente Del Arco cater to various skill levels. You'll find a mix of moderate and difficult routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38139514" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Pizarro loop from Trasierra&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31509907" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgin of Ara – Alcazaba de Reina loop from Guadalcanal&lt;/a&gt; is considered difficult due to its length and elevation.

Q: Are there any specific routes that offer extensive views?
A: Yes, many routes in the mountainous foothills of the Sierra Morena provide scenic vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31509907" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgin of Ara – Alcazaba de Reina loop from Guadalcanal&lt;/a&gt; is known for offering extensive views across the landscape, connecting significant historical landmarks with panoramic scenery.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Fuente Del Arco?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscape, the combination of natural beauty with historical sites, and the variety of routes that cater to different abilities, from gentle Vías Verdes to more challenging mountainous paths.

Q: Is public transport available for cyclists to reach Fuente Del Arco or nearby trailheads?
A: While specific details on bike transport policies for local public transport like Renfe Media Distancia to Fuente Del Arco or Guadalcanal train station are not provided, it's generally advisable to check directly with the transport provider for their current bike carriage regulations before planning your trip.

Q: Are there any cafes or pubs along the touring cycling routes in Fuente Del Arco?
A: The region is dotted with small towns and villages where you can find local establishments. While specific cyclist-friendly cafes are not listed, routes often pass through or near populated areas like Llerena, offering opportunities for refreshments and breaks. It's a good idea to plan your route with potential stops in mind.
tips: The Alcazaba de Reina is an impressive fortress located in the municipality of Reina, near Llerena, in the province of Badajoz. This fortress is located on top of Monte de las Nieves, at an altitude of about 825 metres, offering panoramic views of the Sierra Morena and the Llerena countryside.

The origins of the Regina Turdulorum are unknown but it is believed that its foundation dates from the time of Augustus, linked to the mining exploitation of the area. Its location on the road that linked Augusta Emerita (Mérida) with Hispalis (Seville) surely had very beneficial effects on its development.
